commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: run bot and web server in single asyncio event loop for Linux compatibility

python-telegram-bot v20+ requires signal handlers in the main thread, which
caused a crash when the bot ran in a background thread on Railway (Linux).
Refactored run.py to use asyncio.run() with uvicorn.Server.serve() and the
bot running concurrently via async context manager.

commit.message:
Fix gate redirect loop when localStorage is blocked by browser extensions

Root cause: guard.js on demo pages catches localStorage errors and falls
through to redirect back to gate.html, creating a loop (gate → demo →
guard fails → gate). Fix: when localStorage is inaccessible, allow access
instead of redirecting. Also simplify gate.js back to window.location.replace.

commit.message:
fix: bot echoing raw tool artifacts — deepseek mimicking bracket format

Root cause: when cascading to deepseek, flattenToolExchanges() converted
tool_use blocks to [Used tool: name(input)] format in conversation history.
Deepseek then mimicked this bracketed format in its responses, sending
raw tool artifact text to Telegram instead of actual replies.

Fixes:
- llm-provider.js: Changed flatten format from [Used tool: ...] brackets
  to natural language "(I looked up: name)" — models don't echo this
- index.js: sanitizeOutput returns null (not original text) when response
  is only tool artifacts. Caller skips silently instead of sending garbage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add init/health actions and raw bytes commit to enclave

The parent HTTP server sends { action: "init", lastKnownCounter } on
boot to sync the enclave counter with DynamoDB, and { action: "commit",
bytesB64 } for raw file uploads from the Proof Studio. Both were
unhandled, causing counter rollback errors and "req.digests is not
iterable" failures respectively. Also adds a health action and includes
the esbuild bundling script used for enclave builds.

  to dashboard instead of bouncing through onboarding first

  step 2 after a page refresh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite Tzayad to use foreground sub-agents for data collection

Previously all MCP calls ran in the main thread, consuming the entire
context window before processing could begin. Now data collection
happens in 3 parallel foreground sub-agents (DC-1: Granola/Jira/GitHub,
DC-2: Slack, DC-3: Notion) with isolated context windows.

Also includes fixes from user feedback on the initial seed run:
- Expanded Slack channel list and DM handling (group DMs, 7 contacts)
- Strengthened ownership filter for Act Now items
- Tightened commitment extraction and attribution rules
- 3-month Notion transcript lookback for Darren 1:1s, UJ, R&D Leadership
- Fixed Jira project key (INN -> DATA)
- Added seed mode text for Since Last Run section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sidereal ayanamsa rate, moonrise h0, remove non-Gregorian calendars

- Sidereal (Vedic) zodiac ayanamsa precession rate corrected from 0.0139
  to 1.397 degrees/century (was 100x too small)
- Moonrise/moonset h0 corrected from -0.08 to +0.125 degrees (Meeus standard)
- Removed Hijri, Hebrew, Persian, Ethiopian, Mayan, and Chinese calendar
  systems — kept Gregorian only
- Added .gitignore scoped to Grand Complication files

on Vercel builds. Next.js 15 uses Webpack for production builds.
Also restores middleware.ts (Next.js 15 naming convention).

commit.message:
feat(kernel): AA-03.1 digital twin contract for runtime hot paths (32 tests)

Implement asupersync-1508v.3.4: queueing/network-calculus digital twin
modeling 8 queue stages (ready queue, cancel lane, finalize, obligation
settle, I/O reactor, admission gate, retry backoff, work-stealing) with
service curve types (rate-latency, token-bucket, leaky-bucket, batch),
snapshot field mapping, and disclosed error budgets.

Deliverables:
- docs/digital_twin_contract.md
- artifacts/digital_twin_v1.json (8 stages, 13 snapshot mappings, 3 error metrics)
- scripts/run_digital_twin_smoke.sh
- tests/digital_twin_contract.rs (32 tests incl. network calculus math)

fix: skip customers with invalid email without retrying

HubSpot rejects malformed email addresses (e.g. yahoo.con typos) with
INVALID_EMAIL. These will never succeed on retry, so detect the error
code and skip with a warning instead of queueing a retry and blocking
the customer timestamp update.

commit.message:
Refactor: Update MissionManager extraction status (20% complete)

Updated progress documentation after successful migration of 3 mission functions.

**Summary of Work Completed:**
- 4 critical refactoring tasks complete (error handling, type safety, memory, constants)
- MissionManager extraction 20% complete (3 of 15 functions migrated)
- 75 lines of mission logic moved from game_state.gd to mission_manager.gd
- Dependency injection pattern established and proven
- All call sites updated, backward compatibility maintained

**Functions Migrated:**
1. complete_mission() - Handles ore sales, stockpiling, cargo clearing
2. complete_trade_mission() - Trade mission completion logic
3. calculate_asteroid_intercept() - Pure calculation (3-iteration convergence)

**Remaining Work:**
- 12 functions to migrate (~1320 lines)
- Most are complex creation/redirect/dispatch functions (100-200 lines each)
- All follow established pattern (dependency injection + forwarding stubs)
- Estimated 2-3 days of focused work to complete extraction

**Updated Files:**
- REFACTORING_PROGRESS.md - Detailed status of all migrations

fix: track generated code so Railway build no longer depends on external API fetch

The generated/ directory was gitignored, forcing Railway to fetch OpenAPI
specs at build time. If the fetch failed the binary was never produced,
causing "./hai: No such file or directory" on deploy.

feat: add sleep/wake and settings actions to Dashboard agent rows (#479)

## Summary
- Add inline action buttons (Stop/Wake, Settings) to agent rows on the
Home tab, visible on hover
- Add right-click context menu with Stop, Wake, Wake & Resume, and
Settings actions
- Add 19 new tests covering inline actions and context menu behavior

## Changes
- **Dashboard.tsx** — `AgentRow` component enhanced with:
- Inline action buttons on the right side of each row (appear on hover
via `group-hover:opacity-100`)
- Stop button (running agents), Wake button (sleeping/error durable
agents), Settings button (all durable agents)
- Right-click context menu (`AgentRowContextMenu`) with Stop, Wake, Wake
& Resume, Settings
- Wake fetches durable config via `window.clubhouse.agent.listDurable()`
and calls `spawnDurableAgent`
- Settings navigates to the agent's project view and opens
`AgentSettingsView`
- All actions use `stopPropagation` to avoid interfering with row click
navigation
- `AgentRow` now receives `project` prop from `ProjectCard` for wake
operations
- **Dashboard.test.tsx** — 19 new tests across two describe blocks

## Test Plan
- [x] Sleeping durable agent shows Wake inline button
- [x] Running agent shows Stop inline button
- [x] Durable agent shows Settings inline button
- [x] Quick agent does not show Settings button
- [x] Error durable agent shows Wake button
- [x] Stop calls killAgent
- [x] Wake calls spawnDurableAgent with resume=false
- [x] Settings navigates to agent and opens settings
- [x] Action buttons don't trigger row navigation
- [x] Right-click opens context menu
- [x] Sleeping durable context menu: Wake, Wake & Resume, Settings
- [x] Running durable context menu: Stop, Settings
- [x] Running quick context menu: Stop only
- [x] Wake & Resume calls spawnDurableAgent with resume=true
- [x] Context menu closes on Escape
- [x] Context menu closes after clicking an action
- [x] Typecheck clean, lint clean, all 5481 tests pass

## Manual Validation
1. Go to the Home tab
2. Hover over a sleeping durable agent row — Wake and Settings buttons
should appear
3. Click Wake — agent should start
4. Hover over a running agent — Stop button should appear
5. Right-click any agent row — context menu with appropriate actions
6. Click Settings in context menu — should navigate to agent settings
7. Click Wake & Resume — should wake agent with session resume

---------

Complete Sprint 10: Tech debt cleanup, rule engine expansion, skills, and system documentation

Phase A (tech debt): CentralAgreementConfigs single source of truth, idempotency guard on
recalculate endpoint, FlexEvaluationResponse DTO, smoke test JWT auth, GetDescendantsAsync
single-query optimization.

Phase B (rule engine): CallInWorkRule (minimum hours guarantee), TravelTimeRule (working/non-working
rates), multi-week norm periods (1/2/4/8/12 weeks), part-time pro rata audit (confirmed correct).
18 new wage type mappings. NormCheckRule cleaned up: removed redundant Summarize/NormCheckSummary.

Also adds system documentation, 3 Claude Code skills, and 29 new tests (256 unit + 15 regression).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Document experiments 12-14 (input-readiness gate) in EXPERIMENTS.md

Three iterations of an input-readiness gate for non-leaf refiners were
tried on mng/valgrind-noise (PR #65) to reduce sin benchmark noise under
valgrind serialization:

- Exp 12 (001d579): gate + sub_responded reset. -98% wall-clock but
  +31169% valgrind. Reverted in a47e8d3.
- Exp 13 (cf48fbd): gate + deferred list polling. ~neutral both.
- Exp 14 (5c0bec6, 55d3024): gate + event-driven notification.
  ~neutral both, improved over v2 under valgrind.

Conclusion: approach abandoned. The gate cannot fix scheduling
nondeterminism under valgrind serialization. Further work should target
the benchmarking infrastructure (--fair-sched=yes, min-of-N runs).

docs: comprehensive UI mockup fidelity audit — 23 images vs 17 pages

Full pixel-by-pixel audit comparing all 23 mockup images in
docs/mockups-v3/images/ against all 17 frontend page files.

Key findings:
- 9 pages in GOOD shape (Dashboard, Signal Intelligence, ML Brain,
  Patterns, Backtesting, Data Sources, Market Regime, Settings, Trades)
- 11 pages need PARTIAL fixes (mostly ACC tabs + Sentiment + Performance)
- 1 CONFLICT: SwarmIntelligence.jsx duplicates ACC functionality
- 1 page has NO MOCKUP: CognitiveDashboard.jsx
- Estimated total fix effort: 33-47 hours
- Biggest gap: ACC Swarm Overview tab (card grid vs 12-panel command center)

- docs/MOCKUP-FIDELITY-AUDIT.md — full per-page gap analysis with fix instructions
- docs/STATUS-AND-TODO-2026-03-06.md — current priorities and execution order
